blocks.content: Lena was in the gallery when the call came.

She stepped outside to answer it. She stood on the pavement in front of the building Mr. Alvarez had built and she listened to Ethan's voice and she said I'm on my way right now, and then she ended the call and stood very still.

It arrived whole and without warning — the claw machine, the specific light of that place, Cassandra turning around holding the stuffed bear up with both hands like she had won something worth winning. The look on her face: purely, unguardedly happy. The face of someone who never decided something was too small to enjoy.
The Copper Cup. Her voice through the wall of the back room when things were slow.
Every phone call. The Sunday afternoons. The things they had talked about that Lena had never talked about with anyone else and never would.
She was the first person who had ever looked at Lena and seen what she could become. Not what she was — what she could become. And then treated her accordingly until she started believing it herself.
Lena stood on the pavement and understood, for the first time with the full weight of it, what it meant to lose the person who made you possible.
She stood there for a moment.
Then she went inside to tell Mr. Alvarez.
He took one look at her face and said: Go. Take everything you need.
He didn't ask a single question.
